marketState: neutral-to-bearish - price sits fairly valued but seasonal, storage and technical trends skew lower into late summer
summary: Front price ($2.887) is essentially fair versus the model ($2.917, band $2.417-$3.417), so there is no band-edge extreme to trade off. Seasonality is the bigger tell: price is already 11.9pp below its typical-year path, yet the historical odds still favor further softness (only 31% chance higher in 4 weeks, typical 4-week move -3.1%), albeit with 'Low' reliability. A large bearish storage surprise (+72 Bcf vs forecast, +184 Bcf surplus vs 5y) and uniformly bearish trend/EMA readings across every technical timeframe (50<200 cross on 5m through 1w) reinforce the downside case. The offsetting factor is current weather: 1-7 day demand is running +15% vs 5y at the top of its range, which is near-term supportive even though the 8-16 day outlook and 24h momentum are cooling back toward normal. Managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of its 5y range, leaving room to add shorts rather than signaling an extreme.

longTerm.entry: $2.90 - $2.98 (scale in on a bounce toward the plus7D/14D fair value area)
longTerm.rationale: Seasonality skews down (31% prob higher in 4wk, typical -3.1% move) even though reliability is low, and the +184 Bcf storage surplus with a sizeable bearish build surprise argues for continued oversupply pressure into the fall injection season. Daily/weekly technicals confirm the bearish trend (EMA50<200, bearish trend flag on 1d/1w) and COT shows managed money already net short with room to extend.
longTerm.invalidation: A close back above $3.05, a storage print that flips to deficit-vs-5y, or demand_8_16D pctVs5y turning sharply positive (renewed heat) would invalidate the short thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term technicals are conflicted: 4h RSI (23.3) and 1d RSI (33.6) are already near-oversold with price sitting below the lower daily Bollinger band, while hot 1-7D weather (+15% vs 5y, rangePos 0.78) is providing genuine near-term demand support even as it fades (24h change -14.2). Price is also sitting right on the fair-value now-estimate, so there is no mispricing edge to lean on for a fresh entry.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a bounce into the $2.95-3.00 fair-value zone to sell, or a confirmed break/close below the recent 1d/1w range with RSI resetting off oversold before adding shorts.
